Ad Text:
Leading Financial Services business is seeking a Lead Business Designer to join their team on a day rate contract basis.
 
You will work closely with senior stakeholders and program leadership on a specific program around SDLC and the path to production to enable technology solutions to be developed and delivered at pace.
 

Ensure program and project business solutions draw on best practice and business and process architecture, are informed by customer and business insights, and will achieve the intended outcome
Articulate the end-to-end business context and ecosystem the solution will need to deliver within
Identify and map cross-program dependencies, synergies and opportunities, with a focus on technology solution elements
Ensure alignment across the portfolio of known changes and to strategic objectives and business plans
Work with Program, Project and Change Managers to balance the delivery of the agreed solution in a business and customer centric way
Validation of any technology or business enables the client to achieve the desired business outcomes.
 The right candidate will have:
 

Demonstrated experience working on large scale, multi year, complex programs/projects in excess of $50m annual investment
Exposure to a diverse range of projects e.g. business transformation, application development, process re-design, system replacements, regulatory changes
8+ years in a business environment establishing business value, undertaking root cause analysis, creating operating models, process and/or business architectures, data, customer, colleague and/or service designs
Previous financial services experience
Demonstrated stakeholder management across diverse business groups from C-Suite to subject matter experts and technology representatives
Shaping and definition of complex solutions across products, channels, value chain, business and technology
Demonstrated experience of a Business Partnering mindset and a team player with good business relationship, influencing and collaboration skills
 
The right candidate may have worked as a Business Architect or Management Consultant in the past.
 
Will pay up to $1100 - $1285 per day (inc super) for the right candidate. If you have the right skills and experience, please send your CV now and it will come through to Karen Gregory.

Role type: Full-Time; Continuing
Faculty: Faculty of Business & Economics
Salary: Level of appointment is subject to qualification and experience.
Level C 152,695 - 176,065 AUD p.a. plus 17% super
Level D 183,859 - 202,556 AUD p.a. plus 17% super

Contribute to a premier economics department and shape future economic leaders.


Apply now to join a globally recognised economics department and drive cutting-edge research in econometrics.


Investing in you - benefits package including salary packaging, health and wellbeing programs, discounted services, and professional development opportunities.

The University of Melbourne
We take pride in our people, who all contribute to our mission to benefit society through the transformative impact of education and research. Discover more via our website.

Your next career opportunity
As a Senior Lecturer or Associate Professor in Econometrics at the University of Melbourne, you'll join one of Australia's premier economics departments. You'll have the opportunity to conduct influential research, teach at the forefront of econometrics, and shape the next generation of economic thinkers. Your expertise will contribute to our department's global reputation and impact economic policy and practice.

Your responsibilities will include:

Conduct High-Impact Research: Publish in top-tier journals, secure competitive grants, and engage with international academic networks to advance econometric knowledge.


Deliver Excellence in Teaching: Develop and teach specialist courses in econometrics at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, fostering student success and satisfaction.


Provide Academic Leadership: Mentor junior colleagues, contribute to departmental initiatives, and represent the university in professional forums.


Supervise Research Students: Guide Honours, Masters, and PhD students, creating a rigorous and supportive research environment.

You may be a great fit if:
You are an accomplished econometrician with a strong track record of publications in leading journals, a passion for teaching excellence, and the ability to secure competitive research funding and contribute to the department's international standing

You may also:

Have a PhD in Economics or Econometrics with a focus on advanced quantitative methods.


Possess a robust publication record in top-tier economics and econometrics journals.


Demonstrate experience in securing competitive research grants and supervising postgraduate students.


Have proven teaching excellence at undergraduate and postgraduate levels in econometrics and quantitative methods.


Show a commitment to academic leadership and the ability to contribute to the department's strategic goals.

For further information please refer to the attached PD.

What we offer you!
We offer the opportunity to be part of a vibrant community and enjoy a comprehensive range of benefits to support your success and sense of fulfillment, including:

Supportive flexible work arrangements underpinned by our commitment to inclusion and well-being


Progressive, considerate leave provisions to empower your work-life balance


Salary packaging and access to a range of discounted services including Bupa health insurance


Health and well-being services including a leading Employee Assistance Program

For more information check out our benefits page!

Your new team - Department of Economics
The Department of Economics at the University of Melbourne is a leading economics department in Australia. It has 95 academic staff and is part of a larger community of economists at the university. This includes the Melbourne Business School with 11 academic economists and econometricians and the Melbourne Institute of Applied Economic and Social Research with 43 researchers undertaking research and consultancy projects for government and private sector clients.

The Faculty of Business and Economics holds Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business (AACSB) accreditation and European Quality Improvement System (EQUIS) accreditation. It hosts world class academics and professional staff, the brightest students, alumni, and other stakeholders. We are committed to enhancing our applied research outcomes, expanding our curriculum, and bolstering our connections with industry to help shape the world of business and economics in Australia and internationally.

Be Yourself

We are committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace where all individuals feel valued, respected, and have the opportunity to thrive regardless of gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, age, being a parent or having caring responsibilities.

Help people and communities thrive through human‑centered problem solving.

Struber is growing in Victoria, and we’re looking for a Senior Consultant who thrives in change, brings strong people‑centered consulting skills, and is deeply aligned to our culture and ways of working.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building clarity in ambiguity, working in evolving environments, and partnering closely with clients as they navigate complex change, start‑up conditions, and high‑impact programs.

If you’re energised by meaningful work, value pace and quality, and want to help shape a growing practice – this could be your next move.




About Struber

Struber is a national, human‑based services consultancy. We work across the public and private sectors – particularly infrastructure and service‑delivery environments – helping organisations engage meaningfully, communicate clearly, and manage change in ways that improve experiences for people and communities.

We are values‑led, outcomes‑focused, and proud of a culture that balances pace, clarity, energy and commercial discipline.




About the Role

As a Senior Consultant, you will play a hands‑on role in delivering people‑centered work while helping shape how we grow in Victoria.




You will:




Deliver and lead meaningful work

Lead and deliver people‑focused projects across engagement, communication and change

Work confidently in complex, fast‑moving or start‑up environments

Support clients through sensitive, high‑impact issues affecting communities, customers, staff or partners

Bring structure, rigour and momentum to ambiguous problems

Drive clarity and change

Design and implement communication, engagement and change approaches

Develop messaging, narratives, content, submissions, bids and project materials

Translate technical, policy or program information into clear, accessible and human‑centered outputs

Apply sound planning, risk management and delivery discipline

Build trusted relationships

Partner closely with clients and stakeholders, from community members to senior executives

Facilitate conversations, manage issues, and build shared understanding and alignment

Represent Struber with confidence and professionalism in client environments

Contribute to culture and capability

Coach and support consultants and emerging talent

Role‑model Struber’s Bright Mantras: pace, clarity, energy and commercial discipline

Actively contribute to a positive, inclusive and high‑performance culture

Help shape how we work as the Victorian practice grows




About You




You’ll thrive at Struber if you:

Are comfortable working in ambiguity and change

Bring a start‑up mindset – adaptable, proactive and solutions‑focused

Care deeply about culture, people and impact

Balance human‑centered thinking with commercial awareness and delivery discipline




You’ll likely have experience in one or more of:

Community or stakeholder engagement

Strategic, corporate or program communications

Change management, behaviour change or social impact

Human‑centered design or service design

Bids, tenders, proposals or submissions

Project management in people‑focused environments




You might come from:

Consulting or advisory roles

Government departments or agencies

Infrastructure or service delivery environments

Communications, engagement or change teams

Social impact or community‑focused organisations




Why You’ll Love Working With Us

Meaningful work that improves experiences for people and communities

The opportunity to help shape and grow our Victorian presence

Clear development pathways, including progression to Associate Director

Coaching, mentorship and genuine investment in your growth

A culture that values trust, accountability, pace and shared success

Hybrid working, flexibility and variety across sectors and clients




How to Apply

Ready to make a human impact?

Apply via SEEK or send your CV and cover letter to talent@struber.com.au.

All conversations are confidential.

An established Property, Retail and Services group is seeking a Management Accountant with strong analytical capability to join the business during a period of change and transformation. This role offers strong exposure to senior leadership and long-term career development within a stable, growing organisation.
  
You will be analysing large data sets while also business partnering with operational stakeholders, making sense of the numbers and being able to relay results in simple way non-financial people can understand and use.

Key Responsibilities

Manage income and expenditure across multiple revenue streams including daily postings, reconciliations, analysis, reporting, and recommendation
Look and manage large product data sets providing credible analysis and projections
Knowledge of Power BI very advantageous
Have the ability to communicate complex information in simple terms – both written and verbal.
Prepare monthly management accounts, financial reports, and balance sheet reconciliations
Review and restructure management reports to align with evolving business objectives
Support annual budgeting and forecasting processes
Provide clear financial insights to the CCO, CEO, CFO, and Board
About You

CPA / CA / ACCA qualified (or equivalent) – preferred not essential
A Retail or FMCG background would be advantageous.
Experience supporting financial system or reporting transformations highly regarded
Power BI exposure highly regarded
This is a great role for someone that is looking to stay with the organisation for the long term and is not looking to climb the corporate career ladder and move roles every 12 months. The role will be suit someone who has a strong analytical background who truly wants to become a subject matter expert in a niche field.

This is a close-knit team and has a family feel. Please apply for the role immediately to make your interest known

Project manage high-impact Saas implementations for Government clients.

Drive the roadmap of upgrades and releases.

Hybrid working, supportive team and a pipeline of interesting projects.


About the Role


Insight Data Solutions is a business within a business - using technology to drive efficiency and transparency around land valuations, leveraging new digital capabilities that will minimise bottlenecks and create process efficiencies in delivering valuations for Government entities e.g. council owned properties and land.

Through our unique mix of robust technology and comprehensive industry expertise, IDS enables governments, corporates and consumers to meet their property valuation needs.


As a Project Manager, you'll play a key role in the improvement of Government services through innovation, as we create the national property platform for Government. Working with Product & Engineering teams, you'll drive the successful implementation of VM Online - our Computer Assisted Mass Appraisal (CAMA) software, to Victorian Government from planning to delivery, and support an ongoing roadmap of upgrades and releases with the help of project and support teams.


12 month contract.

Put simply, our Data Solutions division is a true market leader! This is a unique opportunity to join a unique "business within a business", in a very supportive and close-knit culture with a huge pipeline of interesting and varied projects on the horizon to look forward to.


In a typical day you can expect to:


Lead end-to-end delivery of our SaaS implementation projects for government clients - owning scope, timelines, budget and quality while keeping everyone aligned and informed.

Partner closely with Product, Engineering and PMO teams to deliver high-impact outcomes using our hybrid Agile and Waterfall delivery practices.

Act as a trusted client-facing advisor - building strong stakeholder relationships and ensuring each project delivers real business value and ROI.

Drive governance and reporting frameworks that give clarity and confidence to our PMO and Executive teams.

Identify, manage and mitigate risks, issues and change requests - keeping projects moving smoothly and transparently.

Track project performance, financials and success metrics - documenting milestones and ensuring benefits are realised in line with the business case.

Plan and coordinate release activities - managing cross-team dependencies, resolving challenges and communicating release impacts clearly.

Contribute to a high-performing, collaborative team culture - embedding best practice, encouraging engagement and helping shape the future of government property technology.


Why Join Us:


We're the right-sized business for you to make a real impact, with a workplace culture where you can be you. There's so many perks! Here's some of the favourites…


Hybrid working between home, office and visiting clients.
Wellbeing Leave.
Leadership development programs, LinkedIn Learning and more.

Regular social events including our famous Innovation Days and annual BBQ!



Our Ideal Person:


ESSENTIAL:


A Bachelors degree or equivalent from an accredited university or college.

Proficient in Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC).

Experience delivering to Government or large corporate clients.

Experiencing working closely with Product and Technical teams.

Experience with Waterfall, Agile and Lean methodologies.

Superior communication and relationship management skills.

Highly productive, with the ability to lead and work both independently and as a team member.


NICE TO HAVES:


Agile Scrum or similar Project Management certification (preferred).

Company Overview

CoStar Group (NASDAQ: CSGP) is a leading global provider of commercial and residential real estate information, analytics, and online marketplaces. Included in the S&P 500 Index and the NASDAQ 100, CoStar Group is on a mission to digitize the world's real estate, empowering all people to discover properties, insights and connections that improve their businesses and lives.

We have been living and breathing the world of real estate information and online marketplaces for over 35 years, giving us the perspective to create truly unique and valuable offerings to our customers. We've continually refined, transformed, and perfected our approach to our business, creating a language that has become standard in our industry, for our customers, and even our competitors. We continue that effort today and are always working to improve and drive innovation. This is how we deliver for our customers, our employees, and investors. By equipping the brightest minds with the best resources available, we provide an invaluable edge in real estate.


About Domain:

Domain is a leading property technology and services marketplace that is home to one of the largest portfolios of property brands in Australia, including the Domain, Allhomes and Commercial Real Estate (CRE) platforms. In 2025, Domain became part of CoStar Group (NASDAQ: CSGP), a global leader in commercial real estate information, analytics, online marketplaces and 3D digital twin technology. Together, Domain and CoStar Group is dedicated to digitising the world's real estate, empowering all people to discover properties, insights, and connections that improve their businesses and lives.


What's Next?

We'll give your application the thoughtful attention it deserves and get back to you as soon as possible. If there's a match, one of our recruitment consultants will reach out-so keep your phone handy! We're genuinely excited about the chance to work together and make a meaningful impact.


Equity, Diversity & Inclusion

Domain is enthusiastically and unapologetically committed to fostering an equitable, inclusive work culture which reflects our customers and communities. We are proactively looking for candidates from all lived experiences, including people with disability, and people of all ages, ethnicities, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples), faiths, sexual orientations, and gender identities (including trans and non-binary people).

We are committed to providing an equitable recruitment process for people with disability. If you require adjustments during the process we're here to support. If you wish to receive this job advertisement in an accessible format, or have a confidential chat about workplace adjustments, please contact our Equity, Diversity and Inclusion team at edi@domain.com.au or leave a message on 1300 858 356 and we will get back to you.

Company description:



At Stockland we are a community delivering outcomes that benefit the community at large. We work collaboratively and inclusively, building strong working relationships. Our portfolio is diverse and so are the opportunities for professional and career development. We are committed to providing our people with broad experiences to build a successful career.



Job description:



The Opportunity: Be part of a team inspired by possibility.

Join Stockland's Customer & Digital team as a Senior Analytics Engineer, based at our Sydney Head Office. In this pivotal role, you will shape the future of our analytics capability by designing and delivering scalable data models and pipelines that power smarter decision making across the business.

You will lead a small analytics engineering team with 2 direct reports initially, with plans to grow the team over time, and partner closely with stakeholders across Platform Engineering, Business Intelligence, Software Engineering, Finance, Sales Operations and Commercial Property Analytics & Insights.

This is a permanent, full-time role with a hybrid working model. You will be in the office 3 days per week with anchor days on Tuesday and Thursday, and the third day flexible to what works for you and the team.

We're Looking For a...

You are an experienced Analytics Engineer or Data Engineer who enjoys working at the intersection of data, technology and business outcomes. You are comfortable leading others, setting direction for modern analytics engineering practices and working hands on with tools like dbt, SQL and AWS.

You thrive in collaborative environments, enjoy coaching others and are motivated by the opportunity to build and improve an evolving analytics function that has real impact across a large and complex organisation.

What You'll Do

Lead, coach and mentor a team of analytics engineers, creating an inclusive, supportive environment that encourages learning and high-quality delivery.
Scope, design and build robust, reusable data models and transformations using dbt within our AWS ecosystem to support BI, reporting, data science and analytical applications.
Develop and maintain enterprise grade data pipelines and analytical datasets, ensuring strong data quality, testing, documentation and governance.
Translate complex business requirements into scalable, domain driven data models, including ERDs and data flow diagrams aligned to enterprise standards.
Partner with teams such as Platform Engineering, Business Intelligence, Software Engineering, Finance, Sales Operations and Commercial Property Analytics & Insights to continuously improve analytics processes and deliver impactful data products.
Identify opportunities for automation, standardisation and optimisation across the analytics lifecycle, and help set the strategic direction and execution of analytics engineering at Stockland.

About You

You are passionate about turning data into trusted, usable products that enable better decisions. You enjoy working with stakeholders to understand context and needs, and you can explain technical concepts in a clear and accessible way.

You are looking for a role where you can lead a team, shape best practice, and stay close to the hands-on work, while contributing to an organisation that is focused on people, communities and sustainable outcomes.

Experience & Skills That Count

Demonstrated background in Analytics Engineering or Data Engineering, ideally within large or complex enterprise environments.
Strong hands-on experience with dbt and advanced SQL to design and deliver scalable, maintainable data models. dbt experience is essential.
Practical experience working with cloud-based data stacks, preferably AWS, with familiarity in Python, Git and CI/CD considered beneficial.
Proven capability in leading or mentoring technical team members, with strong collaboration, communication and stakeholder engagement abilities.
Comfortable interpreting and designing ERDs, data flows and database schemas, and contributing to improving data processes and standards.

The Right Mindset

A collaborative, inclusive leader who builds strong relationships and enjoys helping others grow.
Curious and improvement focused, always looking for better ways to design, build and deliver analytics solutions.
Comfortable working in an evolving, fast changing environment, with a strong sense of accountability for outcomes and value delivered to the business

The Stockland Proposition

We're a community: a supportive, empowered team that works as one to create better places and experiences for everyone, including ourselves. We're committed to building an inclusive workplace where diverse perspectives are welcomed, and everyone feels a genuine sense of belonging.

If you need adjustments during the recruitment process, just let us know.

Apply Today

Stockland - a better way to live

Corporate Transactions Analyst – Global Real Estate Group

- Corporate finance, capital management and transaction support
- Exposure to senior leadership and deals across Australia and Europe
- Attractive package working for a successful organization $150,000-$170,000 + super

About the company

Global business operating across multiple international markets with a strong focus on strategic growth and capital deployment. The organisation works across complex financing structures, investment activity and corporate transactions, with finance playing a key role in supporting executive decision making. This is a newly created position within the corporate finance function offering direct exposure to senior leadership and the opportunity to work closely with executives across both Australia and Europe. The culture is fast paced, collaborative and commercially focused, with strong visibility across strategic initiatives.

Role summary

Corporate finance role supporting the Head of Corporate Finance across a range of capital management, debt structuring and strategic transaction activities across the group. This position will play a key role supporting capital raising initiatives, fund establishment activity and investment related transactions. The role will involve financial analysis, modelling and coordination of due diligence processes while partnering with finance and investment teams across international offices.

About the role

Support capital raising initiatives and fund establishment activities
Assist with due diligence processes across investment and financing transactions
Monitor debt facilities and support lender engagement and debt management activities
Support BAU capital management initiatives across the group
Deliver financial analysis and modelling to support corporate transactions
Prepare materials and presentations for senior leadership and executive stakeholders
Partner with finance and investment teams across international offices
Support ad hoc strategic finance projects as required
Skills & experience

Degree qualified in Accounting, Finance, Economics or a related discipline
Experience within corporate finance, transactions, funds or investment environments
Exposure to capital raising, fund structures or transaction due diligence highly desirable
Strong financial analysis and financial modelling capability
Confident communicator comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders
Ability to operate in a fast paced, commercially focused environment
How to apply

Click APPLY or contact Charlie Luckett on charlieluckett@charterhouse.com.au for a confidential discussion

www.charterhouse.com.au

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Job Summary / Overview

An experienced Analytics Engineer is required to support asset analysis and modelling through the development of structured data models and reporting solutions. The role focuses on transforming complex SAP PM and CMMS maintenance datasets into reliable analytical outputs that support planners reliability engineers and maintenance stakeholders. This position collaborates closely with developers and operational teams to deliver scalable data solutions and reporting insights.

Key Responsibilities

* Design and maintain curated Snowflake SQL data models and reporting views supporting asset performance and maintenance analytics
* Transform structure and validate SAP PM and CMMS datasets including equipment functional locations work orders maintenance plans and items
* Develop and enhance Power BI datasets and reports to support maintenance strategy and performance monitoring
* Drive continuous improvement in data quality through investigation testing and issue resolution
* Maintain documentation governance and traceability across analytical models and reporting solutions
* Collaborate with technical teams and operational stakeholders to deliver effective analytical outputs
Required Qualifications
* Degree in Engineering Data Science Computer Science or related technical discipline or equivalent industry experience

At Australian Retirement Trust, we’re trusted by millions of Australians to deliver strong long‑term retirement outcomes. Our Investments team plays a critical role in achieving this, and we’re now seeking an Associate Portfolio Manager – Quantitative Enablement to strengthen our data, analytics and quantitative capability.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As an Associate Portfolio Manager - Quantitative Enablement, you’ll support the delivery of high‑quality data pipelines, analytics and technical infrastructure that underpin quantitative research, modelling and investment decision‑making across the Investment business. You’ll work closely with portfolio managers, quantitative researchers and data governance teams to ensure investment data is accurate, robust and fit for purpose.

You’ll join the Defensive and Liquid Assets and Portfolio Intelligence team, responsible for the strategic management of defensive and liquid assets, portfolio completion and opportunistic investments, as well as the data and analytics framework that supports ART’s investment insights and decisions

 

Day to day, you'll:

Perform logical and physical data modelling of investment processes.
Execute a structured data transformation process to deliver data products to investment teams.

Assist in the design and development of quantitative data pipelines and analytical tools to support investment decision‑making.
Implement systems and workflows to ensure the accuracy, completeness and consistency of investment data.
Maintain and enhance investment data quality, documentation and reproducibility.
Contribute to investment data strategy through in‑depth research, complex analysis and actionable insights.
Support standardised workflows to improve efficiency and reduce operational risk in data handling.
Collaborate with Quantitative Research & Analytics and Data Governance teams to align tools and infrastructure with strategic priorities.
Provide technical support to investment teams, including troubleshooting data issues.
Deliver performance updates and insights to stakeholders, contributing to clear and effective reporting and presentations.
Support governance, compliance and risk management obligations across the investment domain.

This role is a 7-month max-term/ secondment opportunity due to long service leave. 

📍 Location: Brisbane or Sydney.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You hold a Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in Business, Technology or a related field, providing a strong foundation for this role.
You bring hands‑on development experience designing, building and supporting data solutions using technologies such as Snowflake, DBT, Python or similar platforms.
You’re experienced working across analytics and software development life cycles and understand how robust processes support high‑quality outcomes.
You have solid experience in the investment industry or a relevant investment domain, with an understanding of market trends and emerging issues.
You demonstrate highly advanced research and analytical skills, enabling you to deliver deep insights that support investment decision‑making.
You’re a strong team player with well‑developed stakeholder management and negotiation skills, and you build trusted working relationships across the business.
You communicate clearly and confidently, and you’re comfortable presenting complex insights to senior stakeholders in both written and verbal formats.
You have experience working in CI/CD environments, using tools such as Azure DevOps and related systems.
You bring development experience with Azure Databricks and Synapse to support scalable data and analytics solutions.

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Job Title: Finance Business Partner - Coffee Sales (12 Month Contract)
Ad Text:
We are Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) - 42,000 people across 31 countries, working together to make, move, and sell some of the world’s most loved beverages. As a leading global consumer goods company, we support 4 million customers and continue to invest in new products and innovation to delight the 600 million people who enjoy our drinks each day. 

In Australia, our portfolio includes the Coca-Cola family of products and well-loved brands such as Sprite, Fanta, Monster and Mother Energy, Mount Franklin, Powerade, Billson’s, and Grinders Coffee. 

About the Role… 

We have a fabulous opportunity to join a collaborative, friendly and successful team in this 12 month contract as Finance Business Partner (Coffee Sales).

Reporting directly to the Associate Director – Coffee Finance, the Finance Business Partner – Coffee Sales will be responsible for providing commercial sales support to the Head of Sales and National Field Sales Manager for our At Home/National and Operational channels along with their wider teams covering National Business Managers and State Managers. The role drives optimised EBIT outcomes and risk mitigation across both channels including our large contracted “Away From Home” customer base, along with contributing to Grinders Coffee long range plan.

 

What You’ll Be Doing...

Partnering with Sales team to deliver Grinders business performance targets through value-add analysis & insights on revenue to EBIT drivers including equipment and capital.

Responsible for preparing and managing the performance reporting processes across Grinders Sales - including month-end commentary, trading updates, reporting & insights.

Responsible for Sales and Operational Planning process in At Home and Away from Home in combination with Sales and Demand Planning teams and with assistance from Senior Analyst, providing risk-adjusted financial analysis and insights.

Responsible for working together with Shared Services Centre to deliver balance sheet management under Claims vs Accruals process providing risk and opportunity analysis across At Home customer accounts, including ensuring correct promo/terms accrual, rebate & pricing set ups.

Deliver commercial review and insights across Grinders customer agreement submissions in conjunction with Sales to maximise financial outcomes whilst identifying risks, recognising and solving contractual hurdles through analysis and modelling.

Influence key operational and strategic decisions through ad hoc analysis and projects.

Work effectively with business leaders to uplift commercial acumen.

Contribute to a high performing, collaborative and empowered team culture.

Collaborate across the Grinders team to help deliver division wide initiatives and projects and provide team coverage and support when required.




The ideal candidate will bring:



Extensive knowledge and understanding of commercial management with a minimum of 3-5 years of experience ideally in an FMCG environment.

Builds and maintains strong working relationships with key stakeholders understanding the objectives of all parties and identifying the associated risk and opportunities.

Strong influencing capability, ability to challenge in constructive manner leveraging data insights and drives mutually beneficial outcomes.

Strong communication and presentation skills with the ability to drive business outcomes and change by partnering with a diverse range of business stakeholders.

Advanced analytical thinking capability with proficient excel skills.

Cross functional experience supporting Sales & Marketing and Supply Chain is preferred.

Strong understanding of financial and commercial drivers as well as understanding of CCEP’s competitive environment.

University degree in Finance, Economics or Commerce related fields required.

SAP / Anaplan experience (prior system experience desired but not essential).

 

What’s in it for you?

Foot in the door opportunity to work with one of the world’s most recognisable brands.

Free monthly product allowances to share with family and friends (including Coca-Cola, Mount Franklin, Monster, and more!).

Access to our corporate benefits including Fitness Passport & discounts with our many diverse customers including Microsoft, Lenovo, Hertz, GoGet and Estee Lauder.

Free counselling for you and your family via our EAP helpline as part of our commitment to well-being.


Applications Close Friday 20th March, 2026




Coca-Cola Europacific Partners is committed to an inclusive, equitable and safe workplace where everyone is supported. If you need any accessibility adjustments during the process, please let us know. Your wellbeing matters to us.  

When you join CCEP, you’re stepping into a global organisation known for developing exceptional talent. We invest deeply in learning, mobility and long‑term growth, creating an environment where your ambition is backed and your career can thrive.

Ad Text:
EPM Solution Architect - Enterprise Transformation

12 Month Contract | Sydney | c.$1,200 per day + GST | Immediate Start

A leading Australian organisation is undertaking a major enterprise-wide transformation program to modernise its financial planning and performance management capabilities. As part of this initiative, an experienced EPM Solution Architect is required to help design and deliver a new integrated Enterprise Performance Management platform.

This program will replace a number of disconnected tools and manual processes with a single, scalable platform that supports enterprise-wide planning, forecasting, budgeting, reporting and analytics.

This is a highly visible role within a complex transformation program and would suit a senior architect who has previously delivered finance or EPM platform implementations within large enterprise environments.

 
Skills & Experience

10+ years' experience in Solution Architecture or Enterprise Architecture roles.
Demonstrated experience delivering or architecting Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) solutions in one or more of the following: 
Oracle Hyperion, Oracle Cloud EPM, SAP BPC, SAP Analytics Cloud, Anaplan, Workday Adaptive Planning, OneStream 
Strong understanding of Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) processes including planning, forecasting and financial modelling.
Experience integrating finance platforms into complex enterprise environments.
Exposure to large-scale transformation programs or finance system modernisation initiatives.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to work across both business and technology teams.
Experience working with system integrators or large delivery partners. 
Contract Details

12 month initial contract
Sydney based
c.$1,200 per day + GST
Immediate start available
This is an excellent opportunity to contribute to a large-scale enterprise transformation program within a high-performing technology and finance environment.

PERSOL is one of Australia's largest and longest serving recruitment providers. Delivering both quality temporary and permanent options, we specialise in the recruitment of Professional, ICT, Government, STEM, Management and Executive talent.

Our Global Client has exciting 6 month contract opportunity for a driven and strategic Senior Business Analyst.

You will be accountable for managing the collation, validation and traceability of business requirements for a complex and new project, from initiation through to completion.

This role will manage and lead business analysis activities, outputs on the project, and will need to steer the direction, quality and outputs of business analysts assigned to the project.

Reporting into the Senior Project Manager, your key responsibilities will include:

Lead the collection, validation, and documentation of core business requirements
Shape and refine requirements to leverage as much Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) functionality as possible
Work closely with stakeholders to challenge legacy systems, processes, and assumptions, focusing on shaping requirements to fit the capability of the new systems
Engage with business units to ensure a clear understanding of the must-have requirements that reflect core business functions
Conduct workshops and meetings to elicit and clarify requirements, pushing for business process alignment with the new systems and eliminating outdated workflows
Work with business units to shape business processes that align with new system functionalities
When deemed necessary, document and map current business processes
Assess the impact of any process changes on the organisational structure, compliance obligations and customer satisfaction
Develop high-quality documentation, including business requirement documents (BRD), process flows, and user stories, ensuring clarity and align
Ensure that business requirements and processes meet all legal, regulatory, and security standards across both the NZ and AU markets
Collaborate with the change management team to ensure that legacy processes are challenged
Mentor and support team members, fostering a collaborative and high-performing environment.

What we are looking for:

Possess advanced BA methodologies, strategically leading and driving best performance while mentoring and creating trusting relationships with all business stakeholders, vendors and project staff.

Strong skills in data analysis tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Tableau, Power BI) are essential for interpreting data and generating insights. As an experienced Senior BA, you will be an analytical problem-solver, clear thinker and excellent communicator and listener.

Expertise in eliciting, documenting and prioritize business requirements will be of an exceptionally high standard. Having worked in effective teams throughout your career, you will be able to adapt quickly and efficiently, creating a winning team environment while sharing knowledge and working collaboratively other BA's working with you.

If interested in this role please apply today or alternatively send your resume to Neema Mehra, neema.mehra@persolapac.com Please note that due to the high volume of applicants only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Brisbane Catholic Education (BCE) is a vibrant learning community within the Archdiocese, employing over 12,000 people across a diverse range of positions. As the largest provider of Catholic education in Queensland, BCE is dedicated to delivering high-quality education from Prep to Year 12. Through strong partnerships with parents, governments, and the wider community, BCE fosters social responsibility, self-discipline and Catholic values in all its students. We offer a supportive working culture where care for the individual is an important part of our ethos. Arrive at work every day to a welcoming and compassionate environment, knowing that you'll be supported to take care of what's important to you. Learn more about Brisbane Catholic Education.



Job description:



Summary

Brisbane Catholic Education is seeking an experienced AI Delivery Lead to lead the strategy and delivery of a system wide portfolio of AI enabled products that improve learning, wellbeing and operational excellence. This senior role is responsible for translating organisational and school needs into practical, human centred AI solutions, while establishing the governance, capability and frameworks required for responsible, scalable and sustainable AI adoption across BCE

Location: BCE Office - Woolloongabba
Work hours: Fulltime | Flexible Working Arrangements Available
Salary: $155,351 - $172,606 plus super
Status: Fixed Term | March - December 2026
Ad Close date: 16 March 2026
Contact: CareersBCE@bne.catholic.edu.au
About the opportunity

In this position you will be:

Lead the strategy, development and delivery of BCE's AI product portfolio, aligned to organisational priorities
Lead cross functional teams across discovery, prototyping, build, deployment, adoption and continuous improvement
Analyse organisational and school needs to inform product roadmaps, prioritisation and decision making
Establish and maintain AI product governance, lifecycle processes, guardrails and assurance checkpoints
Provide expert leadership in AI enabled solution design, responsible AI practices and human centred product development
Build scalable product frameworks and standards that support long term learning, wellbeing and operational outcomes
Partner with and influence senior stakeholders to ensure AI products are embedded effectively across BCE
Manage interdependencies between technology, policy, workforce capability, and schools
Lead workforce and capability uplift in AI, identifying future skills and enabling sustainable organisational growth
About you

To be successful in this position you will bring relevant contemporary skills and experience including:  

Experience leading complex digital or AI enabled products from concept through to delivery and adoption
Being responsible for the implementation and delivery of strategic objectives and attainment
Proven ability to lead and influence cross functional teams and diverse stakeholder groups
Deep understanding of AI governance, risk, ethics and responsible AI practices
A systems thinking mindset, with the ability to design scalable frameworks and standards
Strong analytical skills, using data and insights to guide decisions and prioritisation
A collaborative, human centred approach that supports learning, wellbeing and sustainable change
Tertiary qualifications in a related field and/or equivalent experience in a similar senior role

Positive Notice Blue Card for working with Children

Opportunities for career advancement and professional development

About the role:

As a Data & AI Practice Manager, you provide oversight and mentoring across a multidisciplinary team spanning data engineering, analytics/BI, platform operations, and AI/ML engineering. You will collaborate with other Practice Managers and technical specialists to create consistency in client management, delivery approach, and resource allocation for projects and operational needs. This is a directly chargeable and team leadership role, accountable for client engagement and business development, scoping, solution design, and successful delivery of data platform and AI initiatives.

Drive Comunet's Data & AI Practice and engage in business development.

Maintain personal knowledge and expertise in data platforms, analytics, AI/ML practices, frameworks, and emerging technologies to lead and deliver meaningful change.

Manage consultants and client managers in concert with resource and project managers across Comunet.

Work closely with internal stakeholders, external service providers, clients, and suppliers.

Provide status updates and progress reports as required by leadership team members.

Actively manage a personal portfolio of Data & AI clients and opportunities.




Key responsibilities of the role:

Own the delivery of data platform programs: modernisation, migrations, data integration (ETL/ELT), quality and governance using Data/DevOps practices.

Lead solution scoping and estimation for Data & AI projects, shaping proposals/SOWs, workshops, and delivery plans in collaboration with Project Management.

Guide clients on AI use-cases, feasibility, data readiness, and value realisation; oversee discovery, experimentation, and production phases.

Meet personal and team chargeable targets in line with set budgets.

Drive personal and team consulting capabilities including clear training and development plans.

Influence innovation, architectural and technical design solutions for both Comunet and our clients.

Drive continuous improvement of Comunet engagement and consulting processes, both technical and non-technical.

Engage in relevant vendor or community events and user groups

Be accountable for delegated HR functions for technical specialists as required.

Act as senior point of escalation for data and AI challenges and client issues.

Provide monthly commentary/reporting on team and individual performance, resourcing status/risks, and client portfolio development.




Information Handling Responsibilities:

Understand information management responsibilities associated with the role and adhere to relevant policies and procedures.

Implement data classification, retention, and access controls across client engagements; ensure appropriate handling of PII and sensitive data.

Understand and advise on emergent legislation, guidelines, and practices for Data and AI across security, and ethical concerns.

Promote privacy-by-design and security-by-design as relevant to each client.


Key required skills and qualifications:

A minimum of 10+ years' experience delivering professional IT services, including 5+ years in a combination of Data/Analytics/AI leadership or client management.

You will need to hold an appropriate formal qualification in Information Technology, Data Science or relevant discipline or equivalent experience.

Role will require interstate travel and out of hours commitments.

Proven ability to translate business objectives into Data & AI strategies, roadmaps, and measurable outcomes.

Strong oral and written communication skills for engaging clients, leading workshops, and producing clear documentation.

Effective project management skills: planning, execution, timelines, budgets, and resource coordination in partnership with Project Management.

Leadership and team management: building high-performing teams, fostering collaboration, and aligning goals.

Client Relationship Management: Building and maintaining strong relationships with clients is essential. This involves understanding client needs, providing excellent service, and ensuring client satisfaction

Continuous learning mindset: staying updated with industry trends, technologies, and best practices.

Extensive experience with data platforms (Snowflake, DataBricks, Fabric) and cloud providers (e.g., AWS and Microsoft Azure native data and development services).

Hands-on understanding of data integration (ETL/ELT), streaming, data modelling, warehousing/lakehouse architectures, and BI/analytics (e.g., Power BI).

Experience with data governance and cataloguing (e.g., Microsoft Purview) including lineage, metadata, and policy enforcement.

Exposure to AI/ML engineering: feature pipelines, model training/evaluation, MLOps (MLflow/Azure ML, SageMaker), monitoring and drift management.

Experience in a consulting organisation highly regarded.

Flexible working conditions

Mobility allowance

Training opportunities

Birthday leave

About us: 
Unitywater operates in one of Australia’s most exceptional environments in terms of natural beauty, geographical and demographic breadth. We operate on Kabi Kabi, Jinibara and Turrbal Country, proudly providing safe and reliable water to customers in Moreton Bay, Sunshine Coast and Noosa. We are a water utility serving one of the fastest growing regions in the country. 
We think a pretty good job starts here. 
  
About the role: 
This 19-month opportunity places you at the centre of Unitywater’s economic regulatory framework—where analysis, precision and influence directly shape pricing outcomes and long-term sustainability.
As Regulation Analyst, you’ll support Unitywater’s obligations under the Queensland Competition Authority (QCA), delivering robust, evidence-based pricing submissions and regulatory reporting. You’ll partner closely with finance and operational teams to strengthen cost allocation models, assess regulatory risk, and ensure compliance within a complex, evolving framework.
This is a role for someone who enjoys working where economics, regulation and strategy intersect—bringing clarity to complexity, strengthening transparency, and ensuring pricing decisions deliver sustainable value for customers and communities.
  
About you: 
Our One Unitywater behaviours - Create the Future, Care Together, and Own It – define how we work together at Unitywater. To contribute to this, you will demonstrate the following skills and qualities for the role: The requirements for the position are:
Must Have

Degree and/or Tertiary qualifications in Commerce, Business, Economics or related discipline
Demonstrated experience in economic analysis, financial modelling, regulatory compliance or pricing within a regulated industry
Strong ability to interpret and apply complex regulatory documentation
Proven experience identifying regulatory risks and partnering with leaders to mitigate them
High attention to detail with strong analytical and problem-solving capability
Well-developed written and verbal communication skills, including presenting complex information clearly
Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-moving regulatory environment
Demonstrated ability to build long-term stakeholder relationships and influence outcomes
Self-starter mindset with a proactive approach to continuous improvement
Nice to Have

Membership of a professional body such as CPA or CA
Experience working within economic regulation frameworks similar to the QCA
Exposure to corporate finance and accounting concepts in infrastructure or utilities environments
  
Working at Unitywater:
As a Unitywater team member you will enjoy a range of benefits: 

Flexibility: We value flexibility and support work-life balance with hybrid and flexible working, purchased annual leave and access to floating public holidays. Tell us what this looks like for you! 
Wellbeing: We offer several initiatives to support your wellbeing, such as discounted private health insurance, salary packaging, employee assistance program, and access to retailer discounts and benefits. 
Learning and Development: We offer a range of opportunities to feed your curiosity such as: Walk on Country experience, study assistance, and access to a dedicated learning program and professional memberships. 
Extras: We invite you to join our dedicated employee networks focused on First Nations Reconciliation, LGBTIQ+, CALD, Gender, Life Stage diversity, and Accessibility.  
We celebrate, value and include people of all backgrounds, gender and expressions, sexualities, cultures, bodies and abilities. We are committed to a workforce that reflects the diversity of the community we serve and fostering a workplace that is diverse and inclusive, where you feel connected, are safe to be yourself and supported to be the best version of yourself.
We strive to have a workforce that reflects the diversity of the community we serve and encourage all suitable applicants to apply for this role. Please feel free to share your pronouns and any reasonable adjustments you may need during the interview process. 
If you would like to speak to a team member about our commitment to diversity and inclusion, please contact our Inclusion Specialist emma.guest@unitywater.com or Reconciliation Action Plan Advisor rebecca.williams@unitywater.com.
 
Additional information: 

We are proud to be recognized as an employer of choice for women by WORK180.
We are proud members of Pride in Diversity and Australian Disability Network.  
We are a 2025 Circle Back Initiative Employer – we commit to respond to every applicant.
  
  
  
How to apply:   
Please apply online following the links, and make sure to upload your up-to-date CV. 
If you’re successful we will need you to undergo a few checks….  We know they might not be the fun part, but you will need to confirm your right to work in Australia and complete relevant pre-employment checks, including verification of criminal history, drivers licence and driving history, alcohol and other drug screening. 
 
Applications close:  COB Friday 13th March 

We’re excited to hear from you! Applications will be reviewed as they’re received, and we may close this opportunity early if we find our ideal candidate — so we encourage you to apply soon.
 
For further information please contact the Talent Acquisition team at careers@unitywater.com

Ad Text:
The Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) is an independent Commonwealth statutory authority responsible for the regulation of broadcasting, radiocommunications, telecommunications and some online content.

This work underpins our vision for a connected, informed and entertained Australia.

Who we are

Corporate and Research Division

The Corporate and Research Division partners with business areas across the ACMA, to provide enterprise wide enabling services and strategic advice so that the ACMA can achieve its purpose. The Division facilitates this through the delivery of key corporate functions including financial management, property services, security, procurement advice, performance reporting, research and data, digital and technology services, human resources, communications, and governance services.

Research, Data and Governance Branch

The Research, Data and Governance Branch is at the forefront of shaping and delivering impactful consumer and market research that drives regulatory decisions and supports the ACMA’s compliance and enforcement priorities. We champion the ACMA’s data strategy, data governance and geospatial frameworks, ensuring agency decision-making is supported by high-quality data, sharp analytics, and solid evidence-based insights. The Branch also provides essential governance services, robust risk and fraud management frameworks, seamless parliamentary coordination, efficient internal audit contract management, and targeted international advice and services. The Branch supports the Audit and Risk Committee with secretariat services and oversees the ACMA’s strategic planning and performance reporting.

About the role

Are you interested using authoritative, geospatially enabled data and visualisation to support regulatory outcomes? What about working to ensure that the vision outlined in the ACMA 2024-25 Data Strategy is delivered? 

As the ACMA’s Geospatial Services Manager you will lead the delivery of the ACMA’s geospatial function to meet the analytical and operational needs of the ACMA. 

You will develop and implement work programs applying geospatial and related technologies to the acquisition, integration, production, analysis, and dissemination of geospatial data. This includes leading work programs to enhance and maintain geospatial data infrastructure and to produce geospatial products.

This role requires an experienced geospatial leader who can demonstrate high-level conceptual, analytical, and strategic thinking skills, including the ability to develop innovative and sustainable geospatial solutions. 

Key Duties:

Lead and manage a small team of specialists to delivery projects and support business activity 
Develop and agree a forward work plan that supports organisational priorities and which builds the Agency’s geospatial maturity
Oversee the development and delivery of trusted and authoritative advice and guidance across the geospatial data lifecycle, including consideration of regulatory and legislative implications
Lead the modernisation of enterprise-wide geospatial platforms and data publishing approaches, providing users with more flexible ways to access and use ACMA’s geospatial data
Coach and train other geospatial analysts, and contribute at whole of Agency level to developing geospatial capability
Maintain and manage spatial databases, web services, and Geospatial Information System (GIS) infrastructure (e.g., ArcGIS Server/Portal), automation scripts and applications to improve workflows 
Connect technical and non-technical stakeholders and teams by fostering collaboration and knowledge sharing to leverage geospatial data effectively and unlock its full potential
Represent the ACMA at geospatial and data-related Government fora.

To be eligible for employment with ACMA, applicants must be an Australian citizen. 

The successful candidate will also be required to obtain and maintain a Baseline level security clearance.

Senior Integration & Data Engineers responsible for designing, delivering and maintaining secure Azure-based integration solutions and ETL/ELT pipelines for a leading University in Sydney.

Key Responsibilities

Deliver integration and data solutions aligned with enterprise architecture principles and the University's IT governance model, ensuring scalability, maintainability, and security.
Ensure ETL/ELT processes and data pipelines are reliable, monitored, and meet SLAs while supporting analytics, reporting, and business intelligence needs.
Apply DevSecOps practices to enable secure, automated, and resilient deployment of data and integration services, including infrastructure-as-code.
Support service delivery in line with ITIL practices, focusing on reliability, incident response, change management, and continuous improvement.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, stakeholders, and external partners, and maintain clear system and user documentation for operational continuity.
Provide mentorship and capability uplift to team members while driving continuous improvement through best practices, modern tooling, and automation.

Qualifications, skills & experience

Postgraduate qualification in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline, and equivalent relevant experience, or an equivalent combination of relevant experience and/or education/training.
Preferred: Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or a closely related field.
Proven experience designing and delivering enterprise-grade, cloud-native data and integration solutions on Microsoft Azure.
Demonstrated experience designing, implementing, and governing integration and data solutions in complex enterprise environments using Microsoft Azure services, with strong capability in ETL/ELT, data warehousing, data lake architectures, SQL/NoSQL, and defining data engineering and modelling requirements for analytics and BI.
Hands‑on expertise with Azure Functions, Logic Apps, ADF, APIM, Azure SQL, Data Lake, Service Bus, Event Grid, Azure DevOps, and Microsoft Fabric.
Proficient in Python and C# and experienced with unit testing, automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, DevSecOps, and writing secure, high‑performance code while applying FinOps principles for cost optimisation.
Proven ability to work across complex organisational structures, build strong cross‑functional relationships, apply data‑driven and risk‑based approaches to prioritise engineering activities, drive continuous improvement and innovation, and communicate technical concepts clearly to stakeholders.

Benefits

Fast paced environment with opportunity for professional growth
Generous training and development opportunities
Friendly and collaborative working environment

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

We are seeking an experienced Data Architect / Data Designer to join our team and drive the design and implementation of enterprise data platforms. This role requires strong expertise in Azure, Big Data, and PySpark, with a proven track record in data analysis, modelling, and delivering scalable data solutions.

Key Responsibilities:

Lead data platform architecture design, leveraging Azure, Big Data, and PySpark technologies.
Translate business requirements into data and reporting requirements and implement data designs across delivery sprints.
Conduct source data analysis, data mapping, and data modelling to build Data Hubs and Data Marts that meet business needs.
Work in Data Warehouse (DWH) environments and apply sound knowledge of DWH concepts.
Apply advanced data modelling techniques such as Data Vault, Dimensional Modelling, using tools like Erwin.
Collaborate effectively with business users and technical teams for requirements elaboration, user acceptance, and business change.
Utilize data management tools including Data Governance, Metadata Management, Data Quality, and Master/Reference Data.
Apply strong SQL and data analysis skills to identify patterns and optimize data designs.
Communicate complex data concepts clearly to stakeholders at all levels.
Demonstrate self-motivation, results-orientation, and a drive to continuously improve business processes.

Preferred Experience:

Prior experience in Insurance data domains and business processes.
Experience with Business Intelligence reporting tools and data analysis platforms.

Skills & Qualifications:

Significant experience in data analysis, design, and modelling.
Strong knowledge of Data Warehouse environments and DWH concepts.
Expertise in Azure data services, Big Data ecosystems, and PySpark.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to work independently and lead delivery sprints efficiently.

About the Role:
We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Business Systems Analyst to join our team on a 12-month contract. This hybrid role requires on-site presence in Melbourne three days per week and offers the opportunity to work on impactful system solutions within a dynamic and collaborative environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Deliver Agile business and functional analysis services, contributing to iterative development cycles.
Document business requirements and functional designs using user stories, wireframes, screen mock-ups, and other visual aids.
Facilitate negotiations with business stakeholders, users, technical teams, and product owners to align on solution outcomes.
Lead workshops and engage directly with solution stakeholders to gather and refine requirements.
Perform process re-engineering, capturing both current and future state workflows.
Develop and manage system integration specifications to support seamless technology interactions.
Showcase system solutions and support User Acceptance Testing (UAT) activities.
Key Requirements:

Minimum 3 years of experience in system solution analysis.
Strong background in Agile methodologies and functional analysis.
Proficiency in creating clear and comprehensive documentation.
Excellent stakeholder engagement and negotiation skills.
Workshop facilitation and stakeholder collaboration experience.
Demonstrated ability in process mapping and re-engineering.
Experience with system integration and UAT support.

We're keen to hear from Defence industry professionals with ICT and data experience aligned to the roles listed below, who are interested in permanent consulting opportunities based in Canberra.


Join our Downer Professional Services team and play a key role in delivering complex defence and national security projects that safeguard Australia's future.


Downer Professional Services comprises of three business units our Technical, Advisory, and Capability consulting practice, and Team Downer, which partners with CASG and NSSG as a major service provider to Defence. Together, we are a multi-disciplinary advisory and delivery partner of choice for clients across Defence and National Security.


We're keen to hear from Defence industry professionals with ICT and data experience aligned to the roles listed below, who are interested in permanent consulting opportunities based in Canberra. These roles sit within our Technical, Advisory and Capability business and support our Defence customers, including the Defence Digital Group (DDG) and Joint Capabilities Group (JCG).

Data Governance Lead
Data Governance Analyst
Data Engineers
Data Architects
Data Scientists
Data Resilience
Metadata Specialists
Change Managers
Project Managers

Due to the nature of the work, you are required to currently hold and maintain a minimum Negative Vetting 1 or higher Security clearance as required by the Commonwealth. In some instances, compliance with International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) requirements may also be an inherent requirement.


More than anything, you'll bring a strong work ethic, a positive attitude, and a commitment to delivering high-quality outcomes for our clients.


Why Downer Professional Services


At Downer Professional Services, we're not just a workplace - we're a business dedicated to contributing to the shaping of the future of defence in Australia. As the country's only sovereign prime, we're committed to making a real difference in protecting Australia's interests.


What is means to belong at Downer Professional Services

Meaningful work: Help to deliver critical Defence projects that protect Australia and shape its future.
Professional development: Access career mapping, accelerated learning programs, and shared learning through our Communities of Practice.
Great perks: Enjoy discounts on healthcare, retail, vehicles, and more through Perks@Downer.
Veteran support: Benefit from initiatives like up to 20 days of Reservist Leave to stay connected post-service.

You want your work to matter and so do we. With over 26,000 people across Australia, New Zealand and the Pacific, our team is made up of individuals with unique perspectives, backgrounds, and ideas.


We know that diversity makes us stronger and we actively celebrate it through our commitment to inclusion and belonging.


We're committed to building a team that reflects the diverse communities we serve and we welcome people of all ages, genders, sexual orientations, cultures, abilities, and lived experiences. We especially encourage applications from those whose voices have traditionally been underrepresented in our industry, including women, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples, Māori and Pasifika Peoples, veterans, people with disability, and neurodivergent individuals. Even if your experience doesn't align perfectly with this role, we'd still like to hear from you. If it feels like the right fit, apply - potential counts, and so do you.


As a WORK180 Endorsed Employer, we support flexibility that works for your life, inclusive leadership that values your voice, and equitable access to opportunity so you can do your best work and bring your whole self to it.


If you need support or adjustments through the recruitment process, just let us know, we're here to help you put your best foot forward.

We’ve been proudly serving Australian communities since 1914 and have grown to become a top 30-listed company on the ASX with a portfolio of iconic homegrown brands. At the heart of our continued success is over 115,000 team members working across the country, all contributing to bringing our customers smiles. You’ll not only get to see your work make a difference, you’ll be part of a team working together to help Australians eat and live better every day.

About the team

Strategy is making the future of retail a reality. We’re a big business with big ideas. Our team works together with all our business units to help bring those ideas to life. We stay focused on what’s next, so we can make our customers’ experiences even better now and lead the industry for years to come.

About the role


An opportunity exists for a dynamic Strategy Business Partner (SBP) to join our Group Strategy function. In this pivotal role, you will partner with senior stakeholders to shape and execute Coles’ strategic priorities, ensuring we remain a world class retailer. You’ll play a critical role in turning insights into action, supporting decision-making at the highest level, and driving initiatives that deliver long-term value. Your key responsibilities will include:

Deliver fact-based insights to inform business strategies, the corporate plan, and solutions to critical challenges. 
Partner with Group Strategy Managers and the Heads of Strategy to design and execute Coles’ strategy. 
Apply structured problem-solving and rigorous analysis to develop initiatives and define success metrics for strategic issues. 
Act as a strategic thought partner to senior stakeholders, driving execution of key initiatives. 
Conduct high-quality quantitative and qualitative analysis to solve complex business problems. 
Synthesise findings into clear recommendations that enable effective execution. 
Take ownership of project success within your area of responsibility, ensuring timely and impactful delivery. 
Identify risks to in-scope projects and support the development of mitigation plans.


About you and your skills


You are a strategic thinker with strong analytical capabilities, able to turn complexity into clarity and influence senior stakeholders with confidence. You will bring:



Experience from a leading management consulting (ideally McKinsey, Bain or BCG) or retail strategy.
Demonstrated capability in structured, hypothesis-led problem solving across complex and ambiguous business challenges.
Experience leading workstreams within large-scale, multi-stakeholder transformation or strategy programs.
Exposure to preparing and contributing to Board and executive-level presentations.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence operating at senior leadership level.
Proven ability to collaborate across cross-functional business units within matrixed environments.
A track record of performing in high-performance, outcomes-focused teams.
Strong financial modelling expertise, with the ability to develop robust business cases and commercial insights.
Advanced proficiency in PowerPoint and Excel, with a track record of producing executive-ready materials.

What’s in it for you?

Flexible working options: We know that work is only one part of your life, so we actively encourage a positive work-life balance and provide hybrid working options to help you achieve it.
Office perks: Take advantage of our gym facility and fitness classes, free parking, BBQ area, mini-Coles supermarket, fooderie hub where you can sample new products before they hit the shelves, school holiday program and so much more when you come in.
Discounts: Eligible team members receive 5% discount all year round on your Supermarket and Liquor online and in-store purchases. We also offer additional periods of double discount (10%) at various times throughout the year. As well as hotdeals exclusive to team members that translate into additional savings.
Reward through recognition: Give and receive recognition, linked to our Coles values, through our digital recognition platform ‘mythanks’. You can accumulate points to redeem in the online shop for exciting gifts and electronic gift cards from an extensive range of retailers.
Opportunities for learning and development: No matter where you start within our diverse business, you’ll have experiences, exposure, and education to satisfy you. Discover and explore a variety of career development programs and job-specific training.
Paid parental leave: We understand how important your life outside work is and offer permanent team members paid parental leave to support you in balancing work and family.
Investment in your future: Our annual team member share plan offer allows eligible team members to make regular pre-tax salary sacrifice deductions to purchase Coles Group shares.

About the recruitment process

We’re continuing to build a gender equitable team, and a culture that is just as diverse, inclusive and welcoming as the communities we serve. We are committed to creating a workplace that is safe and respectful for our team. We encourage applications from people of all ages, cultures (including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples), abilities, sexual orientation and gender identities.

We’re happy to adjust our recruitment process to support candidates with disability. For further information and additional contact details visit the ‘Our Recruitment Process’ section of our careers site or email inclusionrecruitment@coles.com.au

Job ID: 177161

Employment Type: Full time


#LI-RM1

Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

We are seeking an experienced QRM (Quantitative Risk Management) functional specialist to join our Treasury Team on a 12-month fixed-term contract. The QRM Treasury Specialist will play a crucial role in ensuring the effective implementation and utilisation of QRM solutions for managing financial risk.

 The ideal candidate will serve as a subject matter expert in implementing and maintaining QRM solutions, bridging the gap between business requirements and technical implementation to optimise our risk measurement and management capabilities while ensuring alignment with regulatory requirements and industry best practices.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s leading purpose-driven bank.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, balancing profit and purpose.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Lead the functional design and implementation of QRM solutions for financial risk management within Treasury, working closely with stakeholders to understand and document business requirements.

At UNSW, we pride ourselves on being a workplace where the best people come to do their best work.  We aspire to be Australia’s global university, improving and transforming lives through excellence in research, outstanding education and a commitment to advancing a just society. 

About the Role

The Business Optimisation Strategy (BOS) is a landmark investment to modernise UNSW’s platforms and simplify service delivery. By streamlining processes and data, BOS enables the "Progress for All" strategy, returning time to staff for teaching and research.

As BOS mobilises, we seek a senior leader with deep expertise in People & Culture service delivery, employment legislation, and enterprise agreements. Reporting to the P&C Program Manager, this role is the accountable expert for ERP and Service Management design, ensuring workforce systems are human-centred, compliant, and efficient.

You will influence program direction, lead HR transformation elements, and ensure the future operating model meets the needs of the UNSW community. This is a rare opportunity to define the employee experience and systems that will underpin talent attraction, payroll, and retention for the next decade. This role is not a project management or business analyst opportunity.

Drive the end-to-end design and delivery of ERP and Service Management capabilities, including Payroll, Recruitment, Talent Management, and Workforce Planning.
Analyze current PeopleSoft configurations and integrations to define and transition to the future state within Oracle Cloud HCM.
Support workstream planning, dependency mapping, and governance reporting in alignment with central program office and EPMO standards.
Collaborate with academic and professional leaders to build consensus on delivery approaches, risk treatments, and P&C service workflows.
Partner with multidisciplinary teams and change management to ensure system integrations, business value, and university-wide adoption are sustained beyond go-live.

Essential: At least 10 years of experience delivering enterprise-wide People & Culture solutions and large-scale ERP transformations, specifically centered on Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM and ServiceNow.
Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline or equivalent expertise gained through executive training and hands-on program leadership in ERP and HCM domains.
Deep expertise in ERP HCM solution design, configuration, and lifecycle management, with a track record of delivering Core HR, Job Architecture, Payroll, Time & Attendance, Rostering & Scheduling, Talent Acquisition, Learning & Development, Workforce Planning, and Performance & Career frameworks. Experience in higher education, healthcare or similarly federated and regulated sectors is desirable.
Proven capability to lead and inspire high-performing, cross-functional teams across business and technology domains to ensure delivery excellence.
Strong engagement and influencing skills, with a record of engaging senior executives and distilling complex ERP data into actionable insights and briefings.
Proven ability to navigate technical complexity and manage dependencies, risks, and trade-offs under conditions of ambiguity and high scrutiny.
Practical knowledge of PeopleSoft HR modules and a commitment to UNSW’s values to drive human-centered change and organizational agility.
